content: Instead of forcing an agent to fit all relevant problem context into its limited working memory for real-time reasoning, we can reframe the agent's reasoning process to offload persistent problem knowledge into a CBR case library, only retrieving and loading the most similarity-matched past cases and their solutions into working memory at each step. This turns working memory capacity from a hard constraint into a controllable bottleneck that aligns with how CBR leverages prior experience.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
